does: This work introduces gpt-oss-safeguard, a prompt-based safety system that enables developers to implement age-specific content moderation for AI applications targeting teenage users. The system provides OpenAI-developed safety policies specifically calibrated to address risks relevant to adolescents, allowing developers to configure moderation rules through prompts rather than requiring custom implementation. The approach operationalizes teen-specific safety considerations by defining what content the system should refuse or handle carefully when interacting with younger users, creating a practical tool for integrating developmental-stage-appropriate safeguards into GPT-based applications.
connects: This work builds on OpenAI's broader safety research and content moderation practices while extending them toward the understudied problem of age-appropriate AI interactions. It responds to growing concerns about AI's effects on minors and gaps in existing moderation systems that typically treat all users identically. The work connects to research on AI safety, content filtering, and responsible AI deployment, while also drawing on developmental psychology insights about adolescent vulnerabilities. It represents a continuation of industry efforts to implement safety measures that go beyond one-size-fits-all approaches, building on prior work in customizable content policies and prompt-based control of model behavior.
enables: This framework enables developers to deploy safer AI systems specifically for teenage audiences, supporting responsible product development in applications where minors are significant users. It sets up future work on systematically understanding and addressing age-specific risks in AI systems, from self-harm and mental health content to social manipulation and misinformation designed to exploit adolescent cognitive development. The tooling could facilitate research into developmental differences in AI risk exposure and enable better evaluation metrics for age-appropriate safety. The approach points toward more granular, demographic-aware safety systems that move beyond binary safe/unsafe determinations toward context and audience-sensitive content handling in generative AI systems.
